channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline to list accomplishments looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek on Monday facing a deadline from President Donald Trump’s cost-cutting chief, Elon Musk, to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump will meet French and UK leaders as uncertainty grows about US ties to Europe and Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump holds talks this week with French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer at a moment of deep uncertainty about the future of transatlantic relations, with Trump transforming American foreign policy and effectively tuning out Europea
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: PUERTO CARTÍ, Panama (AP) — They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic governors balance whether to fight or pacify after Trump threatens one of their own
item.description: President Donald Trump’s real-time confrontation with Maine’s governor over transgender athletes captured the conundrum many Democratic governors are facing in the Republican’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hochul says she made a case to Trump for NYC's congestion pricing, yet predicts courts will decide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul says she told President Donald Trump in a private meeting at the White House that congestion pricing tolls in New York City are necessary and working, yet the Democrat predicted the courts will likely decide the matter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin gets historic 100th World Cup race win and ties record for most podiums
item.description: Shiffrin’s record-extending 100th career World Cup race win Sunday fulfilled a quest put on hold by a serious crash in November.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris receives prestigious Chairman's prize at NAACP Image Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Vice President Kamala Harris stepped on the NAACP Image Awards stage Saturday night with a sobering message, calling the civil rights organization a pillar of the Black community and urging people to stay resilient and hold onto their faith during the tenure of President Do
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Connecticut cannibal killer given conditional release after 2011 murder in which he ate victim's brain, eye
item.description: An institutionalized man who confessed to the 2011 killing of a homeless man in Connecticut in which he also ate the victim's body parts was granted a conditional release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio bathroom law targeting transgender students has brought internal strife to some campuses
item.description: An Ohio law designating school bathrooms for use by people based on their sex at birth kicks in soon.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have until just before Tuesday to explain what they accomplished over the last week.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American Bar Association votes to stop enforcing DEI standard for law schools
item.description: The American Bar Association is suspending its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) standard for law schools amid the Trump administration’s crackdown on DEI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, ABC 4

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Universal reveals Shrek, SpongeBob lands for new theme park in Texas
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences recently revealed several themed areas that will be featured at Universal Kids Resort, a new family-focused theme park set to open next year in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling controversial LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
